metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in Hackney! I've discovered a fantastic range of activities perfect for your 3-year-old, from exploring local parks and museums to Easter-themed events. This weekend is packed with opportunities for hands-on fun, outdoor adventures, and creative play!

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm Easter Activities
activities.description: Free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts at the farm. Meet goats, sheep, and smaller animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for animal lovers, offers hands-on crafts and an engaging Easter hunt, suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Vauxhall, then short walk.
transport.tip: Check the farm's website for specific times of Easter activities.

activities.title: Young V&A Museum
activities.description: Interactive museum with collections of toys, design and childhood objects. The Mini Museum is a sensory space for under 2s.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers interactive exhibits, sensory spaces, and free activities suitable for toddlers. Great for imaginative play and exploration.

transport.tip: Check for specific events and workshops on the day of your visit.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Meet farm animals, participate in gardening activities, and enjoy the community atmosphere.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides close encounters with animals, outdoor space, and opportunities for hands-on learning.

transport.tip: Check for special events or workshops on their website.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play spaces and story worlds, with storytelling sessions, crafts, and singalongs for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers engaging story-based activities, interactive play areas, and a Story Garden playground.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to secure your spot.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme, perfect for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a safe and engaging outdoor play area with a toddler-friendly design.

transport.fromHackney: Tube or bus to Stratford, then walk to Olympic Park.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the Olympic Park's open spaces.

activities.title: V&A Museum of Childhood (Young V&A)
activities.description: Discover toys, games, costumes and childhood objects from the 1600s to the present day. Explore the new galleries – Imagine, Play and Design.
activities.whyItWorks: Imaginative and fun, with lots to spark creativity. Free and indoors, so great for any weather.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Check website for special family events

activities.title: Mudlarks Softplay
activities.description: Indoor soft play based on the history of London's Docks.
activities.whyItWorks: It's an amazing place to learn about history in a child-friendly way.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube or bus to Canary Wharf then walk
transport.tip: Check website for closures

activities.title: Roman London Family Day
activities.description: A free family day exploring the stories of Roman London. Step seven metres below the City streets to discover the stories of Roman London through the Bloomberg writing tablets.
activities.whyItWorks: Children will craft their own stories inspired by ancient scripts found on artefacts at the site.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube or bus to Bank Station
transport.tip: Book a free 30 minute timeslot.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search for giant Easter eggs hidden around the zoo and uncover a secret code.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animal দেখার with an Easter egg hunt, making it engaging for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town, then walk.
transport.tip: Book zoo tickets in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Roam the palace gardens, find Lindt Gold Bunny statues, and win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Appeals to children with a chocolate theme, outdoor exploration, and historical surroundings.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court from Waterloo, then short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Natural History Museum Easter Family Festival
activities.description: Free family-friendly activities and events inside the museum.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines learning with fun, offering interactive exhibits and seasonal activities.

transport.tip: Arrive early to beat the crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum Destination Space
activities.description: Free family shows and early opening times during the Easter holidays.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging for children interested in science and space, with interactive exhibits.

transport.tip: Check the museum's website for show times.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A large playground with a Peter Pan theme, including a pirate ship and sensory trail.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers imaginative play, outdoor space, and sensory experiences suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Notting Hill Gate or High Street Kensington, then walk.
transport.tip: Can get crowded, especially on weekends.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.description: Take part in an Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover facts and unscramble a secret nautical code.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines adventure with history, and chocolate!

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube or DLR to Greenwich
transport.tip: Check website for times

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small, free zoo with rare species of birds and animals, including wallabies and lemurs.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a manageable zoo experience with opportunities to see animals up close.

transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: We're Going On A Bear Hunt
activities.description: A stage adaptation of the classic children's book.
activities.whyItWorks: Great first theatre experience for the little ones

transport.tip: Book in advance

activities.title: Hamleys Easter Events
activities.description: Chef Chocco's Chocolatiers Puppet Show, Hamley's Rainbow ribbon parade, Storytime, Hamley's Dragon Easter egg hunt
activities.whyItWorks: Full of fun for children, but can be very busy

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Spring Family Fun
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families. REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground – a play space made out of repurposed materials (£7.50).
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of choice for different ages.

transport.tip: Check website for details

activities.title: Fairy Tales Exhibition
activities.description: An interactive exhibition exploring enchanted lands, magical creatures and timeless tales through books, costumes, puppets and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for little bookworms

transport.tip: Book in advance

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: A stage production of the classic story.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic tale for all ages

transport.tip: Book in advance

activities.title: The Lion Inside
activities.description: See website for details
activities.whyItWorks: Ages 3 - 7 years

transport.tip: Check website for details

activities.title: Whatever the Weather
activities.description: See website for details
activities.whyItWorks: Ages 0 - 7 years

transport.tip: Check website for details
